events.text: Patient is a 58-year-old male of Asian race, non-Hispanic ethnicity, with a BMI of 24.1 and a past medical history significant for chronic pancreatitis secondary to alcohol use and a 30-pack-year smoking history.

At age 58, patient presented with worsening epigastric pain different from his chronic pancreatitis pain, new-onset jaundice, and pruritus; CT pancreas protocol identified a 2.8 cm mass in the head of the pancreas (C25.0) with involvement of the SMV-portal vein confluence with focal narrowing but patent lumen, classified as borderline resectable.

At age 58, CT abdomen/pelvis with pancreas protocol demonstrated a 2.8 cm mass in the pancreatic head at the SMV-portal vein confluence with focal venous narrowing without occlusion; mild upstream pancreatic duct dilation; moderate intrahepatic biliary dilation; no hepatic lesions; no ascites.

At age 58, ERCP with placement of a 10 mm x 80 mm fully covered self-expandable metal stent for malignant biliary obstruction; pre-procedure total bilirubin 11.2 mg/dL; post-procedure bilirubin improved to 3.8 mg/dL within one week.
At age 58, grade 3 biliary obstruction with total bilirubin peaking at 11.2 mg/dL with associated pruritus, dark urine, and acholic stools; resolved following ERCP and biliary stent placement.

At age 58, EUS examination confirmed a 2.9 x 2.4 cm hypoechoic mass in the pancreatic head with SMV-portal vein involvement; FNA performed with four passes; additional core biopsy obtained for molecular testing.

At age 58, EUS-guided FNA and core biopsy of pancreatic head mass: moderately to poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology code 8140/3) with desmoplastic stroma; tumor cells show nuclear pleomorphism, loss of polarity, and irregular glandular architecture; immunohistochemistry positive for CK7, MUC1, MUC5AC; SMAD4 intact by immunohistochemistry.

At age 58, next-generation sequencing on core biopsy specimen: KRAS G12D mutation (variant allele frequency 28%), TP53 splice site mutation, CDKN2A homozygous deletion, BRCA1 wild-type, BRCA2 wild-type, PALB2 wild-type, ATM wild-type, microsatellite stable (MSS), tumor mutational burden 3 mutations/Mb.

At age 58, CA 19-9 at diagnosis was 624 U/mL; clinical staging cT3N0M0, Stage IIA, borderline resectable; ECOG performance status 0; multidisciplinary tumor board recommended neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX followed by restaging and surgical evaluation.

At age 58, initiated neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX (5-fluorouracil 2400 mg/m2, leucovorin 400 mg/m2, irinotecan 180 mg/m2, oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2) every 14 days; cycle 1 of planned 8 cycles; prophylactic pegfilgrastim administered.
At age 58, after cycle 2 of FOLFIRINOX, patient developed grade 2 nausea and grade 2 diarrhea with 6 loose stools daily; managed with ondansetron, prochlorperazine, and loperamide.
At age 58, after cycle 5 of FOLFIRINOX, patient developed grade 2 thrombocytopenia with platelet count of 62 x 10^9/L; oxaliplatin dose reduced by 25% for subsequent cycles.

At age 58, mid-treatment CA 19-9 after 4 cycles decreased to 198 U/mL from 624 U/mL; patient tolerating treatment with dose modifications; weight loss of 8 lbs since treatment initiation; nutritional support intensified.
At age 58, completed 8 cycles of neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX with dose modifications to oxaliplatin and irinotecan.

At age 58, restaging CT pancreas protocol after neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX showed decrease in pancreatic head mass from 2.8 cm to 1.6 cm; improved fat plane around SMV-portal vein confluence with resolution of focal venous narrowing; no distant metastatic disease; CA 19-9 decreased to 52 U/mL; surgical evaluation recommended.
At age 58, PET/CT showed mildly FDG-avid pancreatic head mass with SUVmax 3.1 (previously 7.4); no distant FDG-avid disease.

At age 58, patient underwent classic pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure) with en bloc portal vein resection and primary venous anastomosis; intraoperative frozen sections of bile duct and pancreatic neck margins were negative; estimated blood loss 500 mL; operating time 6.5 hours.

At age 58, Whipple surgical specimen: residual moderately to poorly differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, 1.1 cm in greatest dimension with extensive treatment effect including 70% tumor necrosis and fibrosis (treatment response grade 2); all surgical margins negative (R0); closest margin at retroperitoneal/uncinate at 4 mm; 0 of 22 lymph nodes with metastatic carcinoma; ypT1cN0M0; lymphovascular invasion absent; perineural invasion present.
At age 58, post-operative course uncomplicated; patient discharged on post-operative day 8; started pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy; pain managed with acetaminophen and short-course tramadol.
At age 59, began adjuvant gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8, 15 every 28 days with capecitabine 1660 mg/m2/day days 1-21 every 28 days for planned 6 cycles.
At age 59, during adjuvant chemotherapy cycle 4, patient developed grade 2 neutropenia and grade 1 fatigue; gemcitabine day 15 dose held; subsequent cycles continued with growth factor support.
At age 59, completed 6 cycles of adjuvant gemcitabine/capecitabine; CA 19-9 normal at 18 U/mL; transitioned to surveillance with CT every 3 months.

At age 59, surveillance imaging and CA 19-9 at 6 months post-adjuvant therapy remain normal; patient counseled on smoking cessation and alcohol abstinence; ECOG performance status 0; weight recovering to near pre-diagnosis baseline.

documents.text:
RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 58, CT abdomen/pelvis with pancreas protocol demonstrated a 2.8 cm mass in the pancreatic head at the SMV-portal vein confluence with focal venous narrowing without occlusion; mild upstream pancreatic duct dilation; moderate intrahepatic biliary dilation; no hepatic lesions; no ascites.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

At age 58, ERCP with placement of a 10 mm x 80 mm fully covered self-expandable metal stent for malignant biliary obstruction; pre-procedure total bilirubin 11.2 mg/dL; post-procedure bilirubin improved to 3.8 mg/dL within one week.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Locally advanced or borderline resectable pancreatic adenocarcinoma. Treated with neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX, some achieving resectability and undergoing Whipple procedure, some with disease progression. Include CA 19-9 monitoring, CT imaging response assessment.
